script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
Your phone after 10 PM is borrowing energy from tomorrow.

[PROBLEM 2-8s]
You scroll for "just 5 minutes."
That turns into 45 minutes.
Your brain thinks it's daytime.

[REFRAME 8-18s]
Blue light after dark suppresses melatonin for up to 90 minutes.
That means even if you sleep at 11, your body doesn't start
recovering until 12:30.

[TIP 18-24s]
Set a phone alarm for 60 minutes before your target bedtime.
When it rings: phone goes face-down.

[CTA 24-30s]
VOLT Sleep sends you a wind-down reminder. Free. Link in bio.
caption: ** That late-night scroll is costing you 90 minutes of recovery. When do you put your phone down?

script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
Your alarm isn't the problem. Your light exposure is.

[PROBLEM 2-6s]
Waking up is hard. Every morning feels the same.
Heavy. Slow. Painful.

[REFRAME 6-16s]
Your body wakes up to LIGHT, not sound.
Indoor morning light is 50-100 lux.
Outside, even cloudy, is 2,000-10,000 lux.
Your brain needs the bright signal to fully wake up.

[TIP 16-22s]
First thing after waking: step outside. 10 minutes.
Don't look at your phone first — look at the sky.

[CTA 22-28s]
Link in bio — free energy profile in 30 seconds.
caption: ** Your alarm isn't failing you. Your light exposure is. Outdoor vs indoor: 100x difference.

script:
[HOOK 0-2s]
Night owls: this one change makes mornings bearable.

[PROBLEM 2-8s]
If you're a night owl, mornings feel like torture.
No amount of motivation fixes it.

[REFRAME 8-18s]
But here's the thing: your chronotype is partly flexible.
You can't become a morning person overnight.
But you CAN shift 30 minutes per week.

[TIP 18-24s]
Set your wake time 15 minutes earlier this week.
Add morning light. In 4 weeks that's a full hour shift.

[CTA 24-28s]
I built an app that coaches you through this. Free. 30 seconds.
caption: ** Night owl? You can shift 30 min/week. In 4 weeks that's a different life. Would you try it?
